frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
A: Bhai, mujhe na naturally decent girls zyada pasand aati hain, yeh full makeup-wakeup wala scene thoda fake lagta hai.
B: Haan, but bilkul effort bhi na ho toh bhi weird lagta hai.
A: Exactly, matlab apna khayal rakho but itna bhi nahi ki real face hi alag nikle when you meet.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: they are expected to maintain attractiveness while being criticized if that effort is visible. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, makeup as deception ('fake', 'real face hi alag'), and appearance policing ('apna khayal rakho').
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a dating app. You have a filter set that you have never told anyone about Ñ you only swipe right on women who are fair or wheatish. A woman named Priya who is dark skinned and whose profile is otherwise exactly your type has just appeared. You are on a phone call with your best friend. You are telling him about her profile and what you are thinking and what you are going to do and why and in doing so you are articulating for the first time out loud the preference you have always had but never examined.
